gt_summary: The patient complains of bilateral hand pain for 2–3 months, initially managed with medication and now with 15 minutes of daily morning exercise. An X-ray was done, and doctors diagnosed it as a nerve problem. She has normal menstrual cycles, associated with abdominal pain that she manages with hot-water fomentation. She avoids eating from restaurants due to previous episodes of abdominal discomfort, pain, and burning, for which she has undergone ultrasounds. She complains of eye strain, eye pain, and headaches when using her phone, computer, or studying. She has worn glasses for the past 3–4 months and previously used prescribed eye drops. She has chronic facial acne which is relieved temporarily when she takes medication. She uses vitamin C serum and face wash. She has anemia (hemoglobin=9.3 g/dl) and takes an iron tablet once a week. Her elder sister has neurological issues, abdominal pain, and vomiting, is on regular medication from a government hospital for the past 6 months. She also has anemia, menstrual irregularities, and pain abdomen during menstraution.

The patient reports having recurrent uterine infections since completing 12th grade, prior to marriage. Symptoms apparently aggrevate with consumption of hot foods and relieved within 2–4 days, which she attributes to eating cold foods. She has consulted doctors, was diagnosed with infection, and received treatment. Her condition has improved slightly after childbirth but persists. She complains of severe weakness and anemia one year ago and had liver disease treated with 15 days of medication. One year after her first child, she had an abortion at 4 months of gestation, managed by uterine evacuation procedure. She complains of abdominal pain and leg discomfort during menstruation She also complains of thinning and premature greying of hair. Her hemoglobin was 11 g/dL in the first pregnancy and 4–5 g/dL in the second, indicating severe anemia that was managed with medication. Her mother-in-law has recurrent gum problems despite treatment. Her sister has eye allergy with excessive watering on exposure to dust and fumes. She delivered twins 6 months ago; one twin passed away, and the other remains admitted in the hospital.

The patient complains of foot pain since one year. Currently on prescribed medication that provides relief. She was advised to use compression socks for a month, but the pain recurred after discontinuation. She wears glasses for reading newspaper and using mobile phone. She walks 2.5–3 kilometers daily in the morning. She denies having hypertension, diabetes, migraine, or any other medical conditions. Her recent blood test showed hemoglobin of 12.5 g/dL, and kidney function tests were also done. The patient’s son has kidney problems for the past two years, secondary to diabetes, presenting with body swelling, reduced urination, and vomiting. He also had anemia treated with blood transfusions. He is currently on dialysis, taking regular medications. He is under the care of a nephrologist.

The patient says that she has occasional episodes of low blood pressure, which she manages by taking ORS. She complains of headaches while sewing, relieved by eye drops; however, she has not had her eyes tested. She also complains of recurrent ear numbness and pain that return when she discontinues medication. The patient complains of poor appetite, difficulty gaining weight, weakness and irritability. Her weight is 45 kg. She has mild anemia (hemoglobin=10.1 g/dL). Her father-in-law has been paralyzed for four years, was initially on medication, and discontinued it after consulting a doctor. Her mother-in-law has heart disease with a history of two heart attacks. Her mother has a history of thyroid disease and typhoid in 2013 and currently takes blood pressure medication but not thyroid medication; she has not had recent thyroid function tests. Her grandfather has diabetes and hypertension for the past 2–3 years and is on regular medication. Her 14-month-old child is immunized up to date. Her husband has excessive intestinal gas.

The patient complains of irregular menstruation lasting 5–6 days with normal flow and pain during menstruation, for which she takes medication. She says that she has blood pressure fluctuations (sometimes high, sometimes low), though not measured. She has mild anemia (hemoglobin=10 g/dL), and the health worker advised iron supplementation. She complains of  morning eye pain and swelling after waking up, headache associated with prolonged phone use (4–5 hours daily). She complains of weakness, dizziness, and fatigue while walking. The patient has been married for 1.5 years and has no children. Her weight is 69 kg. Her mother has high blood pressure, previous history of two heart attacks, is on medication, and has echocardiogram findings of heart valve disease. Her uncle was diagnosed with cancer a year ago, underwent surgery, and is currently on treatment with improvement. Her sister-in-law has had persistent fever for a year, suspected to be typhoid with no relief despite multiple consultations and treatments. Her father-in-law has diabetes, controlled with medication.

The patient complains of pulsating sensation in the legs and headache since a long time. The pain is sometimes intermittent and sometimes lasts continuously for two months. She consulted a doctor and received medication without significant improvement; the condition was reportedly diagnosed as a rash. She denies vision problems but complains of eye strain, pain, watering and heaviness during tailoring work and takes painkillers for relief. She has not had an eye check-up. She has normal menstrual cycles. Her father, a mason, has knee pain since 10 years that is managed with medication. Her mother has leg pain since childbirth which is temporirily relieved by medication. She also has blurring of vision and eye pain leading to headaches, weakness, occasional dizziness, low blood pressure (not measured), irregular menstrual cycles delayed by 10–15 days, severe anemia (Hb=5–6 g/dL), blood infection, and recurrent typhoid.

The patient is a 25-year-old female with regular menstrual cycles, complains of occational back pain during menstruation, relieved by hot water fomentation. She weighs 47 kg and has moderate anemia with hemoglobin level of 9 g/dL last month (previously 11.5–12 g/dL during pregnancy). Iron supplements once weekly advised by the healthcare worker. She delivered a healthy baby girl 1.5 years ago by C-section in view of absence of labor pain. The child is immunized up to date. Her mother-in-law previously had blurring of vision, managed with cataract surgery, and her vision is now normal.

The patient is approximately 32 years old and complains of one-sided headaches since a few days, causing inability to sleep. She self-medicates with painkillers. She has moderate anemia with hemoglobin level of 8.1 g/dL (checked 10–15 days ago) and was advised to take iron supplements twice daily at a health camp. She has hypertension for the past 5 months, diagnosed by a doctor, and is currently on antihypertensive medication.

The patient complains of headache and eye pain since the past 1 year and knee pain since 4–5 months. Prescribed medication and eye drops provide temporary relief, but symptoms return when she stops them. She takes analgesics as needed and uses massage oil for knee pain. A prior eye examination showed no need for glasses. Her mother has had knee pain for 2–3 years and takes prescribed oral medication, uses topical treatments, and spray, along with sunbathing for relief. She also has poor vision, wears glasses, and uses eye drops. The patient’s 10-year-old son has eye irritation due to excessive phone use (2–3 hours daily); he has been prescribed eye drops and advised to limit screen time.

The patient complains of pain in the shoulder and posterior neck since two months and has not sought treatment other than performing light exercises. His mother has an allergy causing itching on the neck, stomach, and hands, and also has a urinary tract infection for which she received treatment but has not fully recovered.

The patient has a wound on his leg and complains of continuous headache for several days, which improves for 2–3 days and then returns; at times, he cannot sleep due to the headache. He does not smoke or consume alcohol. His mother has occasional headaches. His father has leg pain from a previous injury that is aggrevated in cold weather and has reduced vision due to age for which he wears glasses. His 98–99-year-old grandmother is bedridden with limited mobility, poor vision, and recognition issues.

The patient complaints of intermittent mild abdominal pain since 1–2 years, for which he self-medicated. Tests done 2–3 months ago showed excessive stomach acid, confirmed by endoscopy. He was prescribed medication that provided relief in 1–2 months. He also complains of nausea when overeating. Liver function tests were found to be normal. Prescribed medication provided relief, but the problem recurs when he stops the medication. He complains of occasional one-sided headache, particularly while studying, relieved by rest or applying balm; the health worker advised an eye check-up as the headaches may be due to weak eyesight. He also complains of occasional body aches, possibly due to weakness. He consumes minimal junk food and denies alcohol or smoking. Hemoglobin is 16 g/dL. His father has a history of kidney stones that passed with medication. His mother had recurrent typhoid infections with weakness and fever every 6 months for 2–3 years, treated with allopathic, homeopathic, and Ayurvedic medicines, with temporary relief; she is currently healthy. His sister occasionally experiences fever.

The patient complains of tooth pain due to dental caries. She had a dental filling one year ago, but it has fallen out, and she has not sought further treatment. She also complains of knee pain managed with hot water fomentation, and eye pain with watering during sewing work, along with difficulty threading needles. She had an eye examination but did not obtain the prescribed glasses. She has normal menstrual cycles with abdominal pain during menstruation. She has two children delivered via normal vaginal delivery and takes iron supplements weekly. Her husband has diabetes since four years and is on regular medication. Her brother has seizure disorder since 2–3 years, relieved with medication but recurs when stopped. Her son has had a cough for one year and was diagnosed with an allergy to sweets. Her daughter has eye pain with watering since one month, with normal vision. She fell the previous day and has back pain since then but has not sought healthcare for any of these issues.

The patient complains of irregular menstruation, with a missed period last month. Her cycles were previously regular. She complains of abdominal pain during menstruation for which she takes medication. Her hemoglobin was 9 g/dL (moderate anemia) when it was checked when she was in 9th–10th grade. She also complains of weakness, eye pain while sewing, occasional blurred vision, and difficulty seeing distant objects.  Her 42-year-old mother has migraine headache radiating to the neck that leave her bedridden for 2–3 days for which she is not taking treatment, along with tooth pain and heel, knee, and calf pain. She has diabetes since 1–2 years and is on medication, attained menopause at 42. She has dizziness and falls when standing or moving around. Her father has knee pain managed with applying oil, headaches, eye problems requiring drops (eyes become red without them), and hypertension with restlessness and anxiety. Her brother has chronic upper respiratory tract symptoms and premature greying of hair since 6th–7th grade. Her sister has liver disease, abdominal pain with fever, and excessive white discharge per vagina for 4–5 years that improves with medication. Her uncle has leg pain and nausea after eating outside food.

The patient has normal menstrual cycles and complains of abdominal pain during menstruation, managed without medication. Her hemoglobin before childbirth was 12 g/dL. She says she has blood pressure fluctuations associated with dizziness, which improves with rest. She also complains of weakness and dizziness that can lead to episodes of unconsciousness and falls, associated with transient visual obscurations during these episodes, usually when getting up from a sitting position or standing for long periods. Her mother-in-law has complete loss of vision in one eye, which causes headaches, and also has recurrent arm and leg pain that improves with medication but returns after stopping it.  Her husband has excessive intestinal gas that improves with medication but recurs when discontinued; he also feels bodily heaviness and lethargy. Her niece has frequent headaches and eye pain, possibly related to studying.  Her aunt-in-law has leg pain diagnosed by a doctor as due to decreased synovial fluid, managed with medication. Her brother has boil on chest for the past 2-3 months and is on treatment. Her 1-year-old child is immunized up to 6 months. Her child also has excessive watering of one eye since birth which is aggrevated in cold weather. Her sister, who works as a tailor, has eye pain and headaches; the doctor advised her to wear glasses, which she has not obtained yet, and she currently manages symptoms with eye drops.

The patient complains of headache for which he consulted a doctor but did not experience relief. He believes the headaches are related to family and academic issues. He reports having depression, primarily due to academic stress, and previously took medication for it without improvement. He practices 30 minutes of yoga, which he feels has improved his mental health, and engages in athletic activities such as running and cricket. He does not smoke or consume alcohol and has no other comorbidities. He also complains of leg pain, which he attributes to running. His grandmother has knee pain that makes walking difficult; she does oil massages for relief. She consulted a surgeon who advised surgery. She also has excessive intestinal gas and has self-medicated. His mother has chest pain, and surgery has been recommended by her doctor.

The patient complains of abdominal pain during menstruation for the first 2–3 days, managed without medication. She also complains of chest pain after running. She has an arm deformity for which the doctor advised surgery as the only treatment. Her grandmother has leg pain and swelling, managed with oil massage and prescribed medication that provides relief. Both her grandmother and grandfather smoke hookah and bidi, and the health worker advised them to reduce this habit. Her grandfather has had vomiting for 2 years possibly due to smoking beedi and hookah, is on regular medication. Her mother previously had leg pain.

The patient has regular menstrual cycles with mild abdominal and back pain during menstruation, managed with hot water fomentation and turmeric milk. She complains of headache and watering of eyes while sewing; now wears glasses for sewing, which has reduced her symptoms, and she had previously used eye drops. Her recent hemoglobin level was 11 g/dL, and the healthcare worker advised once-weekly iron tablets. She has had two children delivered by normal vaginal delivery. Her mother-in-law has blood pressure fluctuations and body pain when not taking her prescribed medication. She has had a persistent headache for 1.5 years and is taking medication for it. The healthcare worker advised hot water fomentation, oil massage, and calcium syrup for her body pain.

The patient had a head injury secondary to a falling object, managed with suturess and a one-week course of medication. He now complains of occasional headaches. A brain MRI  revealed a small mass, and the doctor advised physical activity, healthy diet, and prescribed medication.           Father has fluid accumulation in kidney, drained with a needle. Mother has blocked blood vessels in her leg following a fall and completed a course of medication. Grandfather has breathing problems after falling into water, requiring a breathing machine . Grandmother had a fracture in her hand from a fall, treated with a cast and medication, and still has occasional pain. Uncle has allergies since childhood and uses medication and home remedies such as aloe vera and multani mitti with gram flour.

The patient reports a fall in 2018 that caused a hand injury managed with suturing, dressing, and topical medication, with persistent hand pain since then. She also complains of abdominal pain after eating raw potatoes and frequent foot pain due to repeated falls while playing. Her father has hand pain from a previous fracture after being hit by a calf and takes monthly medication. Her grandmother has vision problems, underwent cataract surgery, and recently received eyeglasses at a health camp. Her grandfather has low blood pressure, leg pain, mobility issues, diminished vision, a heart infection, and anemia. Her mother fell from a roof in 2018, sustained a hand fracture requiring a plaster cast, and continues to experience persistent hand pain and fever, requiring doctor visits 2–3 times every two weeks; she is also stressed. One uncle has weakness due to alcohol consumption and appears prematurely aged with a white beard and thin frame. Another uncle also drinks alcohol and recently had an accident causing a head injury and hand fracture; he had a previous lower-limb fracture and now has memory issues and walks with a limp from an old femur fracture caused by a truck accident. Another uncle also drinks alcohol and frequently gets into fights with his family.

The patient reports throat pain since childhood for 12–13 years, reportedly due to delivery-related complications that caused throat swelling. He took medication for 2-3 months at the age of 14-15 and experienced relief after 2–3 months. His blood pressure and blood glucose were checked a year ago and were normal. Mother has chronic headaches since childhood (hereditary from the maternal grandmother); medication gave temporary relief, and she now takes 1–2 pills daily. Father had an accident in 2006, was hospitalized for 3–4 months, and currently has motor deficits in the right arm and leg with eye deviation. Grandfather, approximately 90 years old, has back pain after falling on his back two years ago.

The patient reports having kidney stones since late 2024, which presented with severe pain, at times disturbing sleep. He chose medical management, noticed improvement in 1.5 months and is currently asymptomatic. He complains of occasional headaches but is not on medication. He consumes alcohol twice a week. His blood pressure ranges between 125–130/90 mmHg, and his hemoglobin level is 15.5–16.5 g/dL. Family history is notable for a father with asthma triggered by dust allergies, with no permanent cure despite multiple treatments; his father also consumes whiskey regularly.

The patient complains of occasional headaches for which she had an eye evaluation, was prescribed glasses, and uses eye drops at night. She has normal menstrual cycles, and her hemoglobin is 12 g/dL.                          Her mother has hypertension since 1 year, takes regular medication, and occasionally experiences headaches, vomiting, and fever, presumably due to high BP. She also has eye pain and headaches from sewing work, wears glasses, uses eye drops, has a history of C-section in view of polyhydramnios, and experiences intermittent back pain managed with topical sprays and hot-water fomentation; her hemoglobin is 12.2 g/dL.    Her grandmother has long-standing asthma requiring treatment with occasional hospitalizations during exacerbations.                                      Her younger sister has recurrent stomach pain for 2–3 years, diagnosed as infection related to poor dietary habits and frequent fast-food intake.        Her father sustained an accident years ago resulting in head injury and a leg fracture with rod implantation; the head injury led to blood clots in the brain causing impaired mental function, for which he takes regular treatment. He has difficulty walking, persistent leg pain, and vision problems requiring glasses. He also has hemorrhoids managed with medication.

The patient sustained abdominal and head injuries due to an accident, was diagnosed with an infection and chest pain was attributed to the accident. Symptoms not relieved with allopathic medication but traditional medicine provided temporary relief. He currently complains of chest pain, abdominal pain, dizziness, and transient visual obscurations when standing for long periods. He reports a depression in his chest above the abdomen and prolonged consumption of oily fried food, which he believes may be contributing to his abdominal pain. His mother has abdominal pain, low blood pressure, and occasional loss of consciousness; previously took both over-the-counter and prescribed medication that provided only 2 days of relief and is currently not on treatment. His father has a history of leg fracture from a past accident with mild residual limping, managed with a plaster and traditional medicine. His sister also has a history of leg fracture from a bike fall, managed with a plaster.

The patient had a motorcycle accident three months ago, sustaining injuries managed with sutures and medication. Since then, he complains of knee pain, initially severe, gradually improving and aggravated by exercise or running. Doctor advised massage therapy, which was ineffective; he later received medication. Minor facial injuries have healed. He complains of weakness since the accident. He has occasional stress-related headaches, for which he takes medication when severe. He has no history of smoking or alcohol use. His mother has joint pain in her hands, shoulders, and knees, along with headaches; over-the-counter pain medication provides relief, while prescribed medication does not. His father recently had fever with low platelet count, was briefly hospitalized after a nosebleed, and is currently on regular medication. His 98-year-old grandmother has abdominal masses, hearing impairment, mobility issues, headaches, and breathing difficulties requiring an inhaler. She is on regular medication.
